description: After rain, a child picks up smooth stones one by one and places them in a basket. 'nomira' collects selected physical items into an accumulating group, often across more than one act. 'sholei' (gather) brings things or quantities together in one combination; 'nomira' follows the collection as it grows.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper front teeth for voiced dental 'n', let air pass through the nose, and round the lips for middle-height back 'o'. Close the lips for voiced 'm', keep the airflow nasal, and release into stressed high-front 'i'. Make a tap or short trill with the tongue tip for 'r', then finish with open central 'a'.
usage_notes: The collected objects or specimens are the direct object, and the event-noun rule supplies collecting. 'muraki' (harvest) gathers a mature crop, while 'phelure' (store) arranges something for later retrieval. Module 'muphera' (stock) names items already held for future use, and 'sitawi' (inventory) names their record.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Collecting food or useful material asks for knowledge of place and season. 'nomira' names the repeated gathering without deciding that every accumulation was needed.

semantic_domains.activity: selecting and accumulating physical items over one or more acts
semantic_domains.nature: collecting specimens or useful material from a living or physical setting
semantic_domains.physical: bringing tangible objects into an accumulating group
